titleStmt.title: SII 32, part 2, no. 36: original edition by S. Swaminathan
No. 36. (A.R. No. B 268 of 1910.) TIRUVAḌANDAI, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.

summary.p: On the north wall, central shrine, Varāha perumāḷ temple.

It records that the ūrār of Tiruviḍavandai in Paḍuvūr-nāḍu in Āmūr-kōṭṭam received thirty kaḻañju of gold from Nakkan Ēnādi, a resident of Innambar on the northern bank (of the river Kāvēri), in Chōṇāḍu and they (ūrār) undertook to supply ninety nāḻi of oil annually at the rate of one uḻakku per day for burning a perpetual lamp to the god, Maṇavāḷapperumāḷ at Tiruviḍavandai. The ūrār also promised that if they defaulted in supplying the oil they would pay a fine of four and quarter kāṇam at the court of justice and also a mañjāḍi daily to the kō. Further, even after remitting the fine they agreed to measure out oil to the priests of sanctum sanctorum (tiruvuṇṇāḻigai vāriyar) without fail.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. III No. 125.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . kopparakecaripanmarkku yāṇṭu Āṟāvatu Āmūrk koṭṭattup
paṭuvūrnāṭṭu tiruviṭavantai Ūroṅ kaiyeḻuttu coṇāṭṭu
vaṭakarai Innamparkiḻān nakkaṉ Enāti kaiyāl yāṅkaḷ koṇṭu kaṭava po
n muppatiṉ kaḻañcu Ippon koṇṭu kaṭavom Ippoṉ muppatiṉ kaḻañcu ponnukku Ivur
ruṭaiyaRead ivvūruṭaiyamaṇavāḷapperumāḷukku nan=tāviḷakku Oṉṟinukku niccam Uḻakkeṇṇaippaṭi toṇṇūThe length of ū is marked in the next line.
ṟṟu nāḻi Eṇṇai Aṭṭuvomākavum Oṭṭik kuṭuttup pon koṇṭo
m Ipponnāl Eṇṇai can=tirātittavarkkaṭṭuvomākavum poṉ ku
ṭuttu poliyūṭṭu collap peṟātomākavum Oṭṭikkuṭuttom Ū
rom Itaṟṟiṟampil Uṇṭikaiyum paṭṭikaiyum kāṭṭi tanmāsanattile nic
cam nāle kāṟ kāṇam taṇṭam paṭuvomākavum Aṉṟāḷ kovukku nittam mañcāṭi
poṉ manṟupāṭu Iṟuppomākavum It taṇṭamum maṉṟupāṭum Iṟut
tu Ivveṇṇai muṭṭāmait tiruvuṇṇāḻikaivāriyar vacame Eri
kka Aṭṭuvomāka Iṭṭuk kuṭuttom Ūrom .
Digital edition of SII 32, part 2, no. 36 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
